faults.title: Overcurrent
faults.desc: Check motor cables, mechanical load blockage, or short acceleration time.

faults.title: Earth Fault
faults.desc: Check motor cable insulation and motor terminals against ground.

faults.title: DC Overvoltage
faults.desc: Check input voltage, lengthen deceleration time, or use braking resistor.

faults.title: Output Phase Loss
faults.desc: Check motor cable connections at drive terminals (U2, V2, W2) and motor.

faults.title: Heatsink Overtemp
faults.desc: Check cooling fans, panel filter cleanliness, and Ambient temperature.

faults.title: Safe Torque Off (STO)
faults.desc: Check STO safety circuit (XSTO) and ensure both channels are active.

faults.title: Motor Stall
faults.desc: Check for locked rotor or if current limit parameters are too low.

faults.title: Motor Overload
faults.desc: Check for motor overload or motor thermal parameter settings.

faults.title: Input Phase Loss
faults.desc: Check input fuses and stability of main power supply (L1, L2, L3).

faults.title: Comm Loss
faults.desc: Check connection between Control Panel (CDPI) or PC Tool and drive.

faults.title: Ground Fault
faults.desc: Check for current leakage in motor cables or internal motor parts.

faults.title: Short Circuit
faults.desc: Check for short circuits in motor cables or inverter modules.

faults.title: DC Charging Error
faults.desc: Check input voltage or damage to internal charging circuit.

faults.title: Control Panel Loss
faults.desc: Check panel mounting or panel cable if mounted on the panel door.

faults.title: External Fault
faults.desc: Check external signals connected to Digital Input (usually external trip relay).
